blurb: Colorado-native prairie annual — airy wands of pink-purple 'spider' flowers, alive with bees all summer, that reseed into drifting colonies. Still widely sold as Cleome serrulata.
spread: Self-sows freely into colonies; not invasive
seasons: Pink-purple spider-flower heads Jun–Sep; slender dangling seed pods follow; dies at first frost
wildlife: Pollinator powerhouse — native & honey bees, butterflies, wasps, broad-tailed hummingbirds; seeds feed birds
toxic: None of concern — foliage was historically cooked as a potherb ('Navajo spinach')
